title: Radiant Egyptian Penne Pasta
description: Smoky, glassy-jelly sauce inspired by Egyptian flavors, served over al dente penne. A dairy-free, mortar-pestle-pound creation that dazzles the palate.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the penne pasta until al dente, about 10 minutes; drain and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute until fragrant.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika, cumin, and coriander into the skillet, stirring to coat the onion mixture.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, allowing the spices to release their aromas.
Add the chickpeas, tomato paste, and coconut milk, stirring until the sauce thickens and turns a glassy, jelly-like consistency.
Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce simmer for 5 minutes, then season with salt to taste.
Toss the cooked penne pasta into the skillet, ensuring each piece is coated with the smoky sauce.
Finish by squeezing fresh lemon juice over the pasta and garnishing with chopped parsley before serving.
